preamble: Insertion of [ʃ] from zero most often arises through s-retraction in consonant clusters (where /s/ retracts to [ʃ] before certain clusters) or as a secondary consequence of palatalization. True ʃ-epenthesis (insertion with no underlying source) is rare; most documented cases involve a shift of an existing /s/.

examples.note: S-retraction: /s/ in /str/ clusters retracts to [ʃ] — strictly s→sh but perceived as insertion by listeners; well-documented in AAVE and some urban dialects.
OHG initial /sk/ → MHG/ModG /ʃ/ (again s→sh, not ∅→sh; included for completeness as a cluster-initial ʃ development).

sources: Crystal, D. (2008). A Dictionary of Linguistics and Phonetics (6th ed.). Blackwell Publishing.
